mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the average forward return post-trigger is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) combined with rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized demand-pull growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy below 50% over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a sustained monetary tightening cycle where the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, rising borrowing costs compress equity valuations and reduce corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above 3.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, or the forward return differential between high-rate and low-rate regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects rising business and consumer confidence, which translates into immediate positive momentum for equity markets over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The sign of HOUST changes does not correlate with 5-day forward SPX returns above 52% accuracy over a 10-year sample, or the Spearman rank correlation remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index breaks above its 12-month moving average, it confirms a supply-side recovery that alleviates bottleneck constraints, improving corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following the breakout are negative or flat in more than 55% of cases, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.1 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown that suppresses equity valuations and earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeding 3.0% annualized, consumer spending and corporate investment accelerate, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are non-positive in over 55% of quarters where GDPC1 exceeds 3.0%, or the forward return spread between high-growth and low-growth quarters is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
